tips: git status — check the current state of your working tree
Write commit messages in imperative mood: 'Add feature' not 'Added feature'
git diff --staged — review staged changes before committing
git log --oneline -5 — show the last 5 commits compactly
Use .gitkeep files to track empty directories
git commit --amend — update the last commit message or include forgotten changes
git stash — temporarily save uncommitted work
git branch -d <name> — delete a merged branch locally
Keep commits focused: one logical change per commit
git push --force-with-lease — safer force push than --force
git rebase -i HEAD~3 — squash, reword, or reorder the last 3 commits
git cherry-pick <hash> — apply a specific commit from another branch
git bisect — binary search to find the commit that introduced a bug
Use --no-verify to skip pre-commit hooks (use sparingly)
git diff — see unstaged changes before adding them
git add -p — interactively stage hunks of changes
